response.justification: This candidate is a highly experienced senior/staff engineer with nearly two decades of professional work, which makes him dramatically overqualified for an entry-level junior position — a severe mismatch in seniority, compensation expectations, and retention risk. His German is self-reported as basic, while the role requires strong verbal and written German for client-facing insurance consulting. Java appears only peripherally in his history (robotics side work at Altom, listed in skills), with his core depth in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Despite an excellent technical profile and a relevant CS degree, he is the wrong fit for this specific role.
strengths: Completed computer science degree (ETH Zürich) plus deep, broad software engineering experience
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, agile practices, testing culture, and community teaching
Already based in Berlin with extensive experience collaborating across client-facing and product teams
concerns: Only basic German, while the role requires strong verbal and written German for insurance clients
Severely overqualified for an entry-level junior role — high risk of disengagement and early attrition
Java is not a primary working language; recent professional experience centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and agile experience
